reasoning: According to the current 2025 Worldwide Box Office data from Box Office Mojo fetched directly, the highest-grossing film of the year globally is currently *Ne Zha 2*, a sequel to the 2019 Chinese animated film *Ne Zha*, with a staggering $2.26 billion. The next closest is *Zootopia 2* with $1.86 billion, followed by *Avatar: Fire and Ash* at $1.48 billion. It is mathematically almost impossible for a non-franchise film to release this year and eclipse $2.26 billion to take the number one spot—in fact, out of the top ten highest earners currently, none are completely original non-franchise/IP films, with the highest purely original film arguably being *F1: The Movie* at $634 million (which is far below the leader). It's essentially a lock that the highest-grossing film of 2025 worldwide will be a franchise title, likely either *Ne Zha 2*, *Zootopia 2*, or *Avatar: Fire and Ash*, all of which are sequels. Therefore, the probability is set to the maximum allowed of 98%.
